label: Convolutional neural networks

node.label: Convolutional neural networks
node.definition: A convolutional neural network applies shared local filters over grids or signals, building hierarchical features with translation-aware spatial inductive bias.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Convolutional neural networks is modeled as depending on Convolution & local filters, Pooling & local aggregation and feeding downstream nodes such as ResNets & DenseNets, U-Nets & encoder–decoder convnets, Generative adversarial networks.
examples: atlas downstream link to ResNets & DenseNets
atlas downstream link to U-Nets & encoder–decoder convnets
atlas downstream link to Generative adversarial networks
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Convolution & local filters, Pooling & local aggregation.

dependencies.rationale: Convolutional neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Convolution & local filters.
Convolutional neural networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Pooling & local aggregation.
In this atlas graph, Convolutional neural networks has a `composes` dependency on Normalization & standardization with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Convolutional neural networks has a `trained_under` dependency on Supervised learning with `common` strength.
